gene_region.label: PPARG transcribed interval
gene_region.definition: Canonical PPARG genomic interval on GRCh37 / hg19 from Ensembl GRCh37 lookup, aligned to the hg19 coordinate system used by this app.

promoter_review_region.label: Operational promoter review window
promoter_review_region.definition: A practical 1 kb upstream window used by this app to flag promoter-adjacent coverage before the canonical gene interval begins.

promoter_hotspot_region.label: Promoter-associated CpG review window
promoter_hotspot_region.definition: Promoter-proximal CpG-focused span represented by the bundled EPIC subset and used here as the main methylation-review hotspot.

gene_context.gene_summary: PPARG encodes peroxisome proliferator-activated receptor gamma, a ligand-activated nuclear receptor that regulates adipocyte differentiation, lipid storage, insulin sensitivity, macrophage polarization, and metabolic inflammation.
gene_context.clinical_context: The local PPARG bundle is metabolic and pharmacology oriented. Common PPARG markers are low-penetrance modifiers, while rare dominant-negative variants require clinical classification.
variant_effect_overview: Pro12Ala is a widely studied PPARG functional/metabolic marker with context-dependent effects on insulin sensitivity and metabolic traits.
Drug-response interpretation for thiazolidinediones should remain cautious and phenotype/treatment specific.
condition_research_overview: Type 2 diabetes and metabolic trait association research
gene_context.methylation_interpretation: PPARG methylation should be treated as local regulatory context around a nuclear receptor transcriptional control of adipogenesis, insulin sensitivity, lipid storage, inflammation, and thiazolidinedione pharmacology gene. Promoter-proximal methylation can support expression-potential review, but it should not replace genotype, RNA, protein, or phenotype data.
methylation_effects: Promoter or first-exon methylation may suggest a more restrained or permissive local expression state for PPARG.
Because PPARG biology is tissue and pathway dependent, methylation values are context rather than a stand-alone biomarker.

Combined sequence plus methylation findings are most useful when interpreted against nuclear receptor transcriptional control of adipogenesis, insulin sensitivity, lipid storage, inflammation, and thiazolidinedione pharmacology.
methylation_condition_research: Epigenetic regulation studies that use PPARG promoter or gene-body methylation as expression-context evidence.
Variant-plus-methylation review for nuclear receptor transcriptional control of adipogenesis, insulin sensitivity, lipid storage, inflammation, and thiazolidinedione pharmacology.
Type 2 diabetes and metabolic trait association research

evidence.label: Ensembl GRCh37 ENSG00000132170: PPARG coordinate source

biorender_visuals.label: PPARG biochemical figure starter
biorender_visuals.focus: nuclear receptor transcriptional control of adipogenesis, insulin sensitivity, lipid storage, inflammation, and thiazolidinedione pharmacology
biorender_visuals.usage_note: BioRender template and icon-library search results are bundled as figure starters. Open the template link in BioRender, then add or replace the listed icons to make a gene-specific biochemical pathway image.

gene_context.concrete_variant_prediction: The variant observed in this sample suggests a PPARG adipogenesis and insulin-sensitivity thesis: the individual may carry context relevant to lipid storage, adipocyte differentiation, metabolic inflammation, type 2 diabetes traits, or PPAR-gamma agonist pharmacology. Common markers remain probabilistic modifiers.

variant_records.clinical_interpretation: rs1801282 (PPARG Pro12Ala) is curated as a PPARG marker for Adipogenesis and insulin-sensitivity biology, Type 2 diabetes and metabolic trait association research. Read it through genotype dosage, phenotype context, and the nuclear receptor transcriptional control of adipogenesis, insulin sensitivity, lipid storage, inflammation, and thiazolidinedione pharmacology pathway rather than as a stand-alone deterministic result.
variant_records.clinical_significance: Curated research and clinical-context marker; external clinical classification is required for diagnostic use.
functional_effects: Can modify or tag nuclear receptor transcriptional control of adipogenesis, insulin sensitivity, lipid storage, inflammation, and thiazolidinedione pharmacology.
Best interpreted with tissue, ancestry, phenotype, and assay context.
associated_conditions: Type 2 diabetes and metabolic trait association research
research_context: Use this marker as a PPARG pathway-context signal, not as a stand-alone diagnosis.
Sequence, methylation, expression, and protein data may point in different directions and should be reconciled.
variant_records.methylation_interpretation: Pair rs1801282 (PPARG Pro12Ala) with PPARG methylation only as local regulatory context; methylation does not prove the variant's biochemical effect.

literature_findings.genotypes: rs1801282 (PPARG Pro12Ala) or linked PPARG locus genotype states
literature_findings.phenotype: Adipogenesis and insulin-sensitivity biology, Type 2 diabetes and metabolic trait association research
literature_findings.finding: The local curation treats rs1801282 (PPARG Pro12Ala) as a gene-specific marker for Adipogenesis and insulin-sensitivity biology, Type 2 diabetes and metabolic trait association research and nuclear receptor transcriptional control of adipogenesis, insulin sensitivity, lipid storage, inflammation, and thiazolidinedione pharmacology.
